title: Tangy Spicy Polish Buckwheat Crumble
description: A zesty, crispy buckwheat crumble with Sichuan pepper and a lemony tang, perfect for a solo dinner that's Mediterranean-friendly.
instructions: Rinse 200 g of buckwheat groats under cold water and drain well.
In a medium saucepan, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat and toast the buckwheat, stirring until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, Sichuan peppercorns, red pepper flakes, smoked paprika, oregano, and 1 tsp salt. Cook for 1 minute to bloom the spices.
Pour in 250 ml of water, bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the buckwheat is tender and liquid is absorbed.
While the buckwheat cooks, zest one lemon and juice it into a small bowl. Set aside.
When the buckwheat is ready, fluff it with a fork and stir in the lemon zest, lemon juice, and a pinch of extra salt if needed.
Transfer the buckwheat mixture to a baking dish and sprinkle evenly with almond flour.
Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 10-12 minutes or until the almond topping turns golden and crisp.
Remove from the oven, let cool for a few minutes, then serve warm as a standalone dinner.
